Pertinent pre-hardship motions in limine for the cases are discussed by court and counsel. The court will decide whether or not to consolidate Utterback on Tuesday morning (10/11/11). Jury hardshipping will be conducted that day in the afternoon.

The court will need a generic statement of the case from counsel on Tuesday morning. Counsel will not need to be present for jury hardshipping. The motion to consolidate Utterback will be served on defendants in Groups 1 and 2 as well as the Utterback defense counsel.

At 3:30 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

Group 1 will go to trial first, followed by Group 2, Gleason, Cronin and then Utterback (unless it's consolidated).

At 4:15 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, strict liability, false representation and civil battery and granted as to punitive damages. As to negligence and strict liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's purported undisputed fact no. 13 acknowledges that plaintiff's amended responses to special interrogatories identified co-worker Henry Graham and stated what knowledge Mr. Graham had about plaintiff's work with Paco products. A reading of plaintiff's response to the cited interrogatories shows that defendant left out of its separate statement some information known by Mr. Graham that was identified in plaintiff's response. Further, defendant's separate statement failed to include facts and evidence demonstrating that Mr. Graham does not have such knowledge. There is no evidence provided by Mr. Graham's deposition testimony from his own case that he was ever specifically asked about plaintiff. Even if defendant had sustained its burden of production, the declarations of Mr. Graham and Mr. Hawkins and defendant's responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Mr. Graham and Mr. Hawkins are overruled. Defendant failed to submit evidence in its separate statement that made false representation and civil battery issues, other than whether plaintiff established that he worked with defendant's product. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to submit clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression required to warrant punitive damages. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
